notes: FROZEN (applicable: false) — daemon=incapable. On user ESC vibe cancels the agent task and mounts a UI-only InterruptMessage (app.py:2133,2148) but writes NO terminal line to messages.jsonl: the streaming assistant message is appended only after the stream drains (agent_loop.py:1765), so a mid-stream cancel persists at most the user prompt (agent_loop.py:1034,1097). The vibe parser emits turn_done only for a tool-less assistant message and has no IsUserInterrupt path (parser.go:50-74), and the daemon has no working-state idle sweep (pid_manager.go reaps only StateReady/PID=0). So the post-interrupt working->ready settle is architecturally unobservable and the session sticks in working. Same root cause as vibe 2.14 turn-aborted-by-error. Secondary driver=gap:interrupt (scaffold stubs the interrupt/Escape step). No spec phases — nothing recordable until a terminal cancel signal + an idle sweep exist and the driver interrupt step is ported.
